name: @radzor/encryption
description: Encrypt and decrypt data with AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption. Generate cryptographic keys, compute secure hashes (SHA-256/384/512), and handle key derivation with PBKDF2.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: AES-256 encryption key as hex string (64 hex chars = 32 bytes). Use generateKey() to create one.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Output encoding for encrypted data and hashes.

outputs.name: encryptedPayload
outputs.description: Encrypted data with IV and auth tag for authenticated decryption.

fields.name: ciphertext
fields.description: Encrypted data in the configured encoding.

outputs.name: hashResult
outputs.description: Hex or base64 encoded hash digest.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired when an encryption or decryption operation fails.

actions.name: encrypt

params.name: aad
params.description: Optional additional authenticated data (AAD) for GCM.

actions.description: Encrypt data using AES-256-GCM with a random IV.

params.name: aad
params.description: Additional authenticated data used during encryption.

actions.name: generateKey
actions.description: Generate a cryptographically secure random 256-bit key as a hex string.

actions.name: hash
actions.description: Compute a cryptographic hash of the input data.

llm.constraints: No external dependencies — uses Node.js built-in crypto module. Keys must be exactly 32 bytes (64 hex chars) for AES-256. Never reuse an IV. Store encrypted payloads as structured objects (ciphertext + iv + tag). GCM auth tag prevents tampering.
